data.title: Li Auto delivered 30,731 vehicles in July 2025, bringing total cumulative deliveries to 1,368,541.
data.description: Li Auto Inc., a prominent player in China's new energy vehicle sector, disclosed its latest delivery figures. In July 2025, the company successfully delivered 30,731 vehicles. As of July 31, 2025, Li Auto has achieved an impressive milestone, with a total of 1,368,541 vehicles delivered.

data.title: BLUETTI launched Apex 300 after raising $5.4M, leading crowdfunding charts. Flagship portable power station now in retail.
data.description: In Frankfurt, Germany, on August 1, 2025, BLUETTI, a prominent provider of clean energy solutions, revealed the launch of its top product, the Apex 300 Portable Power Station. This came after a successful fundraising campaign on Indiegogo in 2025, where they secured a remarkable $5.4 million in funding, marking it as one of the most funded campaigns of the year.

data.title: Wall Street predicts Chevron (NYSE: CVX) will report Q2 earnings of $1.75/share and revenue of $45.59B, down from previous year. Chevron shares fell 1.3% on Thursday. Exxon Mobil lost dispute over Hess Corp's offshore oil assets in Guyana.
data.description: Chevron Corporation (CVX) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings before the opening bell on Aug. 1. Analysts forecast the Houston-based company to post quarterly earnings of $1.75 per share, a decrease from $2.55 per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be around $45.59 billion, down from $51.18 billion a year earlier. In recent news, Chevron emerged victorious in a dispute with Exxon Mobil over offshore oil assets in Guyana owned by Hess Corp (HESS). Exxon CEO Darren Woods confirmed this development during a CNBC interview on July 18. On Thursday, Chevron's stock closed at $151.64, marking a 1.3% decline. For more details and analyst ratings, interested readers can access the complete story on Benzinga's website.

data.title: Oculis increased loan facility to access up to CHF 100 million from BlackRock, global biopharmaceutical company focused on eye diseases.
data.description: In Zug, Switzerland on August 1, 2025, Oculis Holding AG, a global biopharmaceutical company (Nasdaq: OCS; XICE: OCS), specializing in developing treatments for eye and neuro-ophthalmic ailments, disclosed the modification of its loan agreement with BlackRock-managed entities. The revised loan facility aims to enhance financial support for Oculis' research and development endeavors in addressing critical medical gaps.

data.title: WSPN introduces Global Payment 1.0, API solution for institutional crypto-to-fiat infrastructure.
data.description: Today, in Tortola, the British Virgin Islands, the Worldwide Stablecoin Payment Network (WSPN) revealed the introduction of Global Payment 1.0. This new API-driven payment system is tailored for institutional clients and offers a comprehensive infrastructure for seamless transactions. With this launch, WSPN aims to provide a cutting-edge solution to its clients globally.

data.title: German firms are adopting hybrid clouds for compliance & control; shifting from public clouds to private ones.
data.description: In Frankfurt, Germany, many German companies are transitioning from public cloud services to private and hybrid cloud solutions. This move is primarily driven by regulations and a growing preference for increased control over data management. According to ISG, a leading global technology research and advisory firm, this shift reflects a significant trend in the industry. The move towards private and hybrid cloud solutions has gained momentum as companies aim to navigate the complexities of compliance while maintaining autonomy over their data. It highlights a shift towards more specialized and customizable cloud services tailored to meet the unique requirements of each organization. This strategic shift is important to note as it shows how firms are adapting to regulatory environments and seeking more personalized cloud solutions.

data.title: Dutch housing market hits record-high prices despite interest rate hikes; market remains resilient.
data.description: In the Netherlands, recent reports indicate a surge in house prices to record levels despite forecasts that the post-pandemic boom might wane due to significant interest rate increases from 2022 to 2023 to combat rising inflation. Contrary to expectations, concerns about a drop in demand have not materialized, showcasing the market's resilience. Interest rates are now gradually decreasing from previous highs. The robust economy and limited labor market have played a crucial role in mitigating worries regarding affordability.
